drift_findings: AF003: Baseline count-coherence drift. W1 close reported 421 collected tests. W2 close baseline also reports 421 collected tests, despite adding 6 net-new traceparent tests. Independent probe shows total collection is 427 (with ignores) or 421 (with standard W2 ignores). This suggests the W1 archive's 421 claim already included the 6 traceparent tests (likely because the files were on disk during the W1 run but not yet deliverables) or was misreported. Corrected baseline for W2 is 427 total / 421 filtered.
count_coherence_check: AF003 mismatch noted; 12F/408P/1S counts are consistent across W1/W2 runs, but the underlying collection set has shifted by +6.

AF003.title: Baseline count-coherence drift (421 vs 427)
AF003.description: W1 and W2 both report 421 tests collected in their baseline runs. However, W2 added 6 new tests (3 in test_dispatcher_traceparent.py, 3 in test_router_traceparent.py). Independent audit collection finds 427 total tests. The '421' figure in W2 (and potentially W1) appears to use a stale collection count or an implicit ignore that wasn't updated. The zero-new-failures signal holds, but the numeric baseline is mis-labeled in the archive.
AF003.action_required: None for W2 close; update test-baseline.json counts in W3/W4 to reflect the 427 total.

AF004.title: dispatch.duration_ms measurement-site gap on error paths
AF004.description: On success, duration is sourced from result['wall_clock_seconds']. On DispatchError, it is sourced from a span-local monotonic delta. While acceptable for initial instrumentation, this introduces a theoretical drift between the event log (wall-clock) and the span (monotonic) on error paths. Happy-path drift was measured at 0.11ms; error-path drift is unmeasured.
AF004.target: 0.2.17 test coverage for error-path duration consistency.
